claim_status: Profile/session readiness-review template only; no owner review, no independent review, no privacy review, no executable profile schema, no Space schema, no session schema, no snapshot schema, no migration schema, no migration test claim, no fault test claim, no real-profile fixture approval, no private-session readiness claim, no protected-work readiness claim, no data-loss safety claim, no user-data handling readiness claim, no production profile-format claim, no sync claim, no credential-storage claim, no release-path approval, and no implementation claim.

review_scope.prohibited_placeholder_policy: Template records must not leave null fields in a real review or name placeholder schema packages, reference platforms, fixture policies, owner reviewers, independent reviewers, privacy reviewers, title-only approval, self-approval, real-profile fixture approval, or implied owner authority.

format_axes.required_evidence: Executable profile schema with schema_version, profile identity, privacy mode, policy references, storage root handle, downgrade policy, repair policy, deletion policy, export policy, checksum or integrity marker, and owner review beyond the checked no-claim profile/session readiness-review template.

Executable Space schema with profile binding, Space identity, layout generation, tab references, export policy, cross-profile rejection, and owner review beyond the checked no-claim profile/session readiness-review template.

Executable session schema with windows, tabs, navigation entries, lifecycle state, protected-work markers, checkpoint sequence, previous checkpoint hash, and owner review beyond the checked no-claim profile/session readiness-review template.

Executable snapshot schema with retention class, redaction policy, restore limits, content manifest hash, privacy boundaries, export policy, and owner review beyond the checked no-claim profile/session readiness-review template.

Executable migration schema with migration ID, source and target schema versions, step sequence, rollback plan, resume token, failure reason, and owner review beyond the checked no-claim profile/session readiness-review template.

behavior_axes.required_evidence: Disk-full tests prove write reservation, previous checkpoint preservation, visible recovery, and no unapproved deletion.
Power-loss tests prove ordered checkpoints, replay journal behavior, resume token use, hash verification, and idempotent restart.
Corruption tests prove malformed, truncated, duplicated, reordered, and checksum-mismatched records are rejected or quarantined.
Downgrade tests prove older binaries cannot silently write newer formats and that export or upgrade guidance is safe.
Export tests prove redaction, retention, credential exclusion, private-session exclusion, and user-visible scope controls.
Deletion tests prove explicit confirmation, revocable lifecycle where possible, audit records, and no cross-profile deletion.
Private-session tests prove data remains memory-only and never enters normal profile, session, snapshot, export, trace, or diagnostic records.
Crash-recovery tests prove checkpoint restoration, protected-work preservation, renderer crash separation, and no unsafe page-effect replay.
Protected-work tests prove unsaved form state, downloads, uploads, and pending consequential agent actions are not silently discarded or replayed.
Privacy tests prove credentials, cookies, raw page content, private-session data, and sensitive diagnostics remain excluded or redacted.
Data-loss tests prove interruption, rollback, repair, quarantine, deletion, export, protected-work, and recovery behavior before any data-loss safety claim.

migration_axes.required_evidence: Source records are classified without opening real user profile data outside an owner-approved fixture policy.
Input validation covers schema version, identity fields, checksums, privacy labels, downgrade policy, bounded sizes, and hostile records.
Migration plans include target schema, steps, rollback plan, protected-work handling, privacy review, unsupported boundaries, and owner review.
Temporary snapshots are bounded, checksummed, redacted, retained only as allowed, and cleaned up.
Commit happens only after verification succeeds; failures roll back to the previous readable state without discarding protected work.
Repair is bounded to supported cases and corrupt, downgrade, private-session, or ambiguous records are quarantined.
Privacy and cleanup checks prove private-session exclusion, fake credential use, redacted diagnostics, temp-root cleanup, and no real user data retention.

fixture_policy_axes.required_evidence: Real user profile data remains prohibited until owner-reviewed privacy and fixture policy approval exists.
Fake profile roots, IDs, sessions, snapshots, migration records, and rollback fixtures are used for tests.
Fake credentials and credential handles prove no credential material enters schemas, traces, exports, or diagnostics.
Private-session fixtures prove exclusion from profile, session, snapshot, export, sync, traces, and diagnostics.
Temporary roots are bounded, isolated, destroyed after tests, and cannot point at real browser profiles.
Cleanup evidence proves temporary roots, migration artifacts, exported packages, and diagnostic bundles are removed or retained by policy.

privacy_axes.required_evidence: Credential values never enter profile/session/snapshot/migration schemas, traces, diagnostics, exports, or fixtures.
Private-session data is excluded from durable stores, sync, export, crash recovery, snapshots, and diagnostics.
Raw page content is excluded unless a reviewed local-only protected-work rule explicitly allows bounded retention.
Export redaction is tested for credentials, cookies, page content, private-session records, and sensitive diagnostics.
Diagnostics use IDs, hashes, reason codes, and redacted fields instead of user data or secrets.
Retention expiry is required for snapshots, temporary migration data, diagnostic bundles, and exported evidence.
Sync, collaboration, account, and cloud behavior remains unsupported until separately reviewed and tested.

owner_review_axes.required_evidence: Storage owner accepts executable schemas, migration behavior, safe failure, and unsupported boundaries.
Privacy-data reviewer accepts fixture policy, data minimization, redaction, retention, and real-profile prohibition or approval.
Product reviewer accepts private-session, protected-work, export, deletion, recovery, and user-visible failure behavior.
Security reviewer accepts hostile-input handling, downgrade behavior, credential exclusion, integrity checks, and quarantine behavior.
Quality reviewer accepts malformed-input, interruption, rollback, repair, fault, migration, and cleanup test evidence.
Release-operations reviewer accepts downgrade, migration, rollback, support, and release-path implications.
Production-readiness reviewer accepts data-loss, real-profile, user-data, support, and migration implications before production format claims.
Documentation, readiness, task, crosswalk, risks, requirements, repository map, and validation records are updated after review.

rejection_rules.condition: This checked no-claim profile/session readiness-review template is cited as completed readiness evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject profile/session readiness, user-data handling readiness, data-loss safety, production profile-format, release-path approval, and implementation claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Schema package, reference platform, fixture policy, owner reviewer, independent reviewer, or privacy reviewer is null, placeholder, title-only, self-approved, or unqualified.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner review, privacy review, and readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Profile, Space, session, snapshot, or migration executable schemas are missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject schema readiness and production profile-format claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Migration tests do not cover classify, validate, plan, temporary snapshot, commit or rollback, repair or quarantine, privacy, and cleanup stages.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject migration readiness and real-profile migration claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Evidence covers success paths without malformed, interrupted, rollback, repair, downgrade, privacy, and data-loss cases.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject data-loss safety and user-data handling readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Schemas or migrations can silently discard protected work, unsaved form state, downloads, uploads, or pending consequential agent actions.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject protected-work and data-loss safety claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Private-session data can enter normal profile, session, snapshot, export, trace, diagnostic, sync, or migration records.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject private-session readiness and privacy claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Corrupt, truncated, duplicated, reordered, oversized, downgrade, or checksum-mismatched records are accepted as successful input.
Real user data, real browser profiles, real credentials, or production profile roots are used before owner-approved fixture policy exists.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject fixture policy and privacy review claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Sync, collaboration, account, cloud, or multi-device behavior is claimed from profile/session schema evidence.
Credential storage, credential safety, autofill, account, or secret handling is claimed from this template or schema evidence.
Privacy-data review is missing, stale, self-approved, or not tied to retained evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject real-profile, user-data, export, diagnostic, and production profile-format claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Passing repository validation or a no-claim template is cited as proof of product data safety, production support, release readiness, or implementation completion.
A document removes unsupported behavior, residual risk, claim boundary, no-claim wording, or prohibited claim language without replacement evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the change and keep PB-016 partial.
